step_by_step_thinking: I agree that adjusting the cusps of the opposing teeth (Option A) is appropriate for addressing a premature contact in centric occlusion. This approach allows for fine-tuning the relationship between the maxillary and mandibular posterior teeth where their primary points of contact occur during maximum interclosure. Disagreeing on option B about mesial/distal positional changes is valid because such alterations might be less precise compared to modifying individual cusp heights, especially if only centric occlusion needs adjustment while lateral or protrusive functions remain normal.
raw_response:
I agree that adjusting the cusps of the opposing teeth (Option A) is appropriate for addressing a premature contact in centric occlusion. This approach allows for fine-tuning the relationship between the maxillary and mandibular posterior teeth where their primary points of contact occur during maximum interclosure. Disagreeing on option B about mesial/distal positional changes is valid because such alterations might be less precise compared to modifying individual cusp heights, especially if only centric occlusion needs adjustment while lateral or protrusive functions remain normal.

Answer: A.
